params.name: delivery_success
params.description: Filter events by whether all webhooks were successfully delivered. If false, events which are still pending or have failed all delivery attempts to a webhook endpoint will be returned.

params.name: ending_before
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `ending_before` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, starting with `obj_bar`, your subsequent call can include `ending_before=obj_bar` in order to fetch the previous page of the list.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: limit
params.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.

params.name: starting_after
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `starting_after` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with `obj_foo`, your subsequent call can include `starting_after=obj_foo` in order to fetch the next page of the list.

params.name: type
params.description: A string containing a specific event name, or group of events using * as a wildcard. The list will be filtered to include only events with a matching event property.

params.name: types
params.description: An array of up to 20 strings containing specific event names. The list will be filtered to include only events with a matching event property. You may pass either `type` or `types`, but not both.

items.docs: List events, going back up to 30 days. Each event data is rendered according to Stripe API version at its creation time, specified in event object api_version attribute (not according to your current Stripe API version or Stripe-Version header).

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

items.docs: Retrieves the details of an event. Supply the unique identifier of the event, which you might have received in a webhook.
